ogTitle: Cranbourne East vs Cranbourne North: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cranbourne East and Cranbourne North.
heading: Cranbourne East vs Cranbourne North — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cranbourne East, Victoria and Cranbourne North, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cranbourne East has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Cranbourne North (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cranbourne East or Cranbourne North safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cranbourne East has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Cranbourne North scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cranbourne East scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Cranbourne North scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cranbourne East or Cranbourne North?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cranbourne East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
